title: keyring: blocking OS keychain calls made directly in async context
description:
## Problem
KeyringBackend::get() and put() call keyring::Entry::get_password() and set_password() directly in async fn bodies without spawn_blocking. These are blocking synchronous calls to the OS keychain (macOS Security.framework, Windows Credential Manager, Linux D-Bus). Blocking inside an async fn holds the tokio executor thread for the duration of the call, which starves other tasks.

## Comparison
FileBackend correctly wraps all blocking FS calls in spawn_blocking. KeyringBackend does not.

## Risk
- High: on macOS, Keychain calls can block for hundreds of ms waiting for user authorization prompts or daemon IPC
- Medium: on Linux, D-Bus calls can block waiting for gnome-keyring-daemon
- Low: on Windows, Credential Manager calls are usually fast but can block on slow storage

## Fix
Wrap both get_password() and set_password() calls in tokio::task::spawn_blocking.

## Acceptance
Code uses spawn_blocking for all keyring I/O. cargo test -p secretx-keyring passes.
